gaps: MISSING: Custom EQ preset persistence after saving and re-opening the EQ screen (not just tab close/reopen, but full navigation out and back into the EQ screen). TC 65641574 tests 'closing and reopening the EQ tab' but the bug is about saving a Custom EQ preset, then navigating away (Home screen) and coming back — this is a deeper lifecycle path than just tab close/reopen.

MISSING: Save button interaction as a state trigger. No test verifies that tapping 'Save' actually persists the custom slider values to the device/app storage such that they survive a full navigation exit (Home screen) and re-entry into the EQ screen.

MISSING: Custom EQ vs Preset asymmetry. Existing tests (65641573, 65641574, 65641575, 65641576, 65641578) test preset persistence (Bass, Flat, Bright) but do NOT specifically test Custom EQ preset persistence. The bug is specific to Custom EQ — the behavior may differ from preset persistence because Custom EQ involves saving arbitrary slider positions.

MISSING: Slider value persistence within Custom EQ. No test validates that after saving a Custom EQ with specific slider positions (e.g., Bass +5, Mid -3, Treble +2), those exact values are restored when re-opening the EQ screen.

generated_testcases.title: TC_CUSTOM_EQ_001: Custom EQ preset persistence after full navigation exit and re-entry
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name (e.g., 'Poly Voyager Free 60+ UC').
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap 'Custom' preset to enter Custom EQ editing mode.
Set slider positions to: Bass = +5, Mid = -3, Treble = +2.
Tap the 'Save' button to save the Custom EQ preset.
Tap the Home/Back button to navigate to the main Home screen of the app.
Wait 2 seconds.
Tap the connected device name again.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the current preset selection and slider positions.
generated_testcases.expected: The Equalizer screen should show 'Custom' preset as selected. The slider positions should display Bass = +5, Mid = -3, Treble = +2, exactly as saved. The values must not have reverted to defaults or to any other preset values.

generated_testcases.title: TC_CUSTOM_EQ_002: Custom EQ persistence after app backgrounding and foregrounding
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap 'Custom' preset.
Set slider positions to: Bass = +7, Mid = 0, Treble = -4.
Tap 'Save' button.
Press the Home button on the phone to background the app.
Wait 5 seconds.
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app from the app switcher or home screen.
Navigate to the connected device → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the preset selection and slider values.
generated_testcases.expected: The Equalizer screen should show 'Custom' preset selected with Bass = +7, Mid = 0, Treble = -4. The values must persist through the app backgrounding cycle.

generated_testcases.title: TC_CUSTOM_EQ_003: Custom EQ persistence after app force-kill and relaunch
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap 'Custom' preset.
Set slider positions to: Bass = +3, Mid = +5, Treble = -2.
Tap 'Save' button.
Fully close/kill the Poly Studio Mobile app from the recent apps list (swipe away).
Wait 3 seconds.
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for the app to connect to the device.
Navigate to the connected device → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the preset selection and slider values.
generated_testcases.expected: The Equalizer screen should show 'Custom' preset selected with Bass = +3, Mid = +5, Treble = -2. The Custom EQ values must survive a full app kill and relaunch.

generated_testcases.title: TC_CUSTOM_EQ_004: Custom EQ persistence after Bluetooth disconnect and reconnect
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap 'Custom' preset.
Set slider positions to: Bass = +6, Mid = -5, Treble = +4.
Tap 'Save' button.
Navigate back to the Home screen.
Disconnect Bluetooth from the phone's Bluetooth settings or turn off the device.
Wait 5 seconds.
Reconnect Bluetooth (turn device back on or reconnect from Bluetooth settings).
Wait for the app to detect and reconnect to the device.
Navigate to the connected device → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the preset selection and slider values.
generated_testcases.expected: The Equalizer screen should show 'Custom' preset selected with Bass = +6, Mid = -5, Treble = +4. The Custom EQ values must persist through a Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect cycle.

generated_testcases.title: TC_CUSTOM_EQ_005: Save button as explicit state trigger — verify persistence without navigating away
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap 'Custom' preset.
Set slider positions to: Bass = +8, Mid = +2, Treble = -6.
Do NOT tap 'Save'.
Tap the Home/Back button to navigate to the Home screen.
Tap the connected device name again.
Navigate to Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the preset selection and slider values.
generated_testcases.expected: The Equalizer screen should NOT show the unsaved Custom values. It should either show the previously saved preset (if any) or the default preset. The unsaved slider positions (Bass=+8, Mid=+2, Treble=-6) must NOT appear, confirming that tapping 'Save' is the required state trigger for persistence.

generated_testcases.title: TC_CUSTOM_EQ_006: Custom EQ vs Preset asymmetry — verify Custom EQ survives preset switching and return
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap 'Custom' preset.
Set slider positions to: Bass = +4, Mid = -2, Treble = +6.
Tap 'Save' button.
Switch to a different preset (e.g., 'Bass').
Switch back to 'Custom' preset.
Observe the slider positions.
generated_testcases.expected: When switching back to 'Custom' preset, the slider positions should show Bass = +4, Mid = -2, Treble = +6 — the exact values that were saved. The Custom EQ values must not be overwritten or reset when switching to another preset and back.

generated_testcases.title: TC_CUSTOM_EQ_007: Custom EQ slider boundary values persistence
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap 'Custom' preset.
Set slider positions to their maximum values: Bass = +10, Mid = +10, Treble = +10.
Tap 'Save' button.
Navigate to Home screen.
Navigate back to the connected device → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the slider positions.
Now set slider positions to their minimum values: Bass = -10, Mid = -10, Treble = -10.
Tap 'Save' button.
Navigate to Home screen.
Navigate back to the connected device → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the slider positions.
generated_testcases.expected: First observation: All sliders should show +10 (maximum values) after saving and re-entering. Second observation: All sliders should show -10 (minimum values) after saving and re-entering. Boundary values at both extremes must persist correctly.

generated_testcases.title: TC_CUSTOM_EQ_008: Custom EQ persistence after phone reboot
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected device name → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Tap 'Custom' preset.
Set slider positions to: Bass = +2, Mid = -1, Treble = +3.
Tap 'Save' button.
Navigate to Home screen.
Reboot the phone.
After phone restarts, open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for the app to connect to the device.
Navigate to the connected device → Audio Settings → Equalizer.
Observe the preset selection and slider values.
generated_testcases.expected: The Equalizer screen should show 'Custom' preset selected with Bass = +2, Mid = -1, Treble = +3. The Custom EQ values must survive a full phone reboot.
